content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic motivation mechanisms (from Topic A's ICM-based autonomous curious agents) can be repurposed to solve the 'dead-end exploration problem' in modern coding agents, where current systems get stuck repeating incorrect debugging iterations when faced with unfamiliar codebase patterns or emerging edge requirements. By rewarding coding agents for exploring unvisited code branches and undocumented dependency interactions instead of only rewarding task completion, we can create self-improving coding systems that continuously expand their problem-solving scope without human-labeled training data.
